page: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual server featuring an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor. It is configured with 8 shared vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 16 MB of L3 cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 350 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier for most standard operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1437.59, while the multi-core score reaches 11603.93. Memory bandwidth is measured at 831.82 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 62.89 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The server demonstrates average capabilities in static web serving at 8.38 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 12.74 million operations per second. However, it exhibits performance limitations in database operations, scoring a weak 2589.14 on Passmark database tests, and struggles with medium-sized LLM inference, yielding a poor 5.01 tokens per second for 7B model text generation.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-effective option for workloads that require high-speed NVMe storage and moderate multi-core processing without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. It is well-suited for static web serving, caching layers, and lightweight text or file compression tasks. It is less appropriate for intensive database management systems or complex machine learning inference due to the shared resource model and lower performance in those specific benchmarks.
description: The Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is an x86_64 High Performance virtual server powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 8 shared vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a single-core stress-ng score of 1437.59 and a multi-core score of 11603.93. The server excels at static web serving and Redis SET operations, though it shows performance trade-offs in database operations and medium-scale LLM inference. This configuration represents a resource-dense, qualitatively cost-efficient option for web serving, caching, and general-purpose development workloads that benefit from fast NVMe storage but do not require dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server featuring 8 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced performance for web serving and caching.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is an Intel Xeon virtual server with 8 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe storage, optimized for general web workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server combining eight Intel Xeon vCPUs with fast NVMe storage for web serving and caching workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory and 350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Average performance in static web serving and Redis SET operations
Best suited for web serving, caching, and lightweight development tasks
